---
type: entity
created: 2026-06-15
updated: 2026-06-15
sources:
  - "[Statement-GDC-WSC_March-2024](../sources/Statement-GDC-WSC_March-2024.qmd)"
  - "[News-ITU-COP30_GDA-Hub-2025](../sources/News-ITU-COP30_GDA-Hub-2025.qmd)"
  - "[itu-t-l-1480-greening-by-digital](../sources/itu-t-l-1480-greening-by-digital.qmd)"
  - "[ITU-T L.1470 (Greening Digital)](../sources/itu-t-l-1470-greening-digital.qmd)"
  - "[ITU-T L.1501 (Adaptation Best Practices)](../sources/itu-t-l-1501-adaptation-best-practices.qmd)"
  - "[ITU-T L.1500 (Adaptation Framework)](../sources/itu-t-l-1500-adaptation-framework.qmd)"
tags:
  - "organization"
aliases:
  - "ITU"
  - "International Telecommunication Union"
  - "ITU-T"
  - "ITU"
  - "International Telecommunication Union"
---

## Related Entities
- [World Standards Cooperation](world-standards-cooperation.qmd)
- [international-organization-for-standardization](international-organization-for-standardization.qmd)
- [International Organization for Standardization](international-organization-for-standardization.qmd)
- [World Summit on the Information Society](world-summit-on-the-information-society.qmd)
- [Green Digital Action Hub](european-green-digital-coalition.qmd)
- [COP30](cop30.qmd)
- [United Nations](united-nations.qmd)
- [GSMA](gsma.qmd)
- [Coalition for Digital Environmental Sustainability](coalition-for-digital-environmental-sustainability.qmd)
- [ITU-T Study Group 5](itu-t-study-group-5.qmd)

## Related Concepts
- [International Standards](../concepts/international-standards.qmd)
- [Global Digital Compact](../concepts/global-digital-compact.qmd)
- [Sustainable Development Goals](../concepts/sustainable-development-goals.qmd)
- [Green Digital Action](../concepts/green-digital-action.qmd)
- [Digital Cooperation](../concepts/digital-cooperation.qmd)
- [Greening by Digital](../concepts/greening-by-digital.qmd)
- [Recommendation ITU-T L.1480](../concepts/recommendation-itu-t-l-1480.qmd)
- [ITU-T L.1470](../concepts/itu-t-l-1470.qmd)
- [Climate Change Adaptation](../concepts/climate-change-adaptation.qmd)

## Mentions in Source
- "The world’s leading developers of international standards – the International Electrotechnical Commission, the International Organization for Standardization, and the International Telecommunication Union – provide a trusted platform for digital cooperation among governments, industry, academia, and civil society." 
- "Unveiled on COP30's opening day, the hub is set to become the nerve centre of Green Digital Action, a partnership kicked off by the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) with leading tech organizations at COP28 in 2023." 
- "The International Telecommunication Union (ITU) is the United Nations agency for digital technologies, driving innovation for people and the planet with 194 Member States and a membership of over 1,000 companies, universities, civil society, and international and regional organizations."
- "The International Telecommunication Union (ITU) is the United Nations specialized agency in the field of telecommunications, information and communication technologies (ICTs)."
- "The ITU Telecommunication Standardization Sector (ITU-T) is a permanent organ of ITU."
- "ITU-T is responsible for studying technical, operating and tariff questions and issuing Recommendations on them with a view to standardizing telecommunications on a worldwide basis."
